title: Ntangki National Park
tagline: Ntangki National Park — wildlife reserve of Nagaland

overview.short: Ntangki National Park is a national park located in Peren District of Nagaland, India.
overview.description: Ntangki National Park is a national park located in Peren District of Nagaland, India. It was first designated as a national park in 1993. Among the species that inhabit the park are the rare hoolock gibbon, golden langur, hornbill, Asian palm civet, black stork, tiger, white-breasted kingfisher, monitor lizard, python and sloth bear. The name "Ntangki" is derived from the Zeme dialect of the Zeliangrong Nagas.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ntangki National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ntangki National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ntangki National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ntangki National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ntangki National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ntangki National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Ntangki National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ntangki National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are New Soget, Gopibung, Lower Sinjol, Songngou, Phaikolum.
